description:
You are assisting an automation-workflow specialist who needs a quick proof-of-concept for “JSON schema validation plus jq data extraction”.

Pre-created resources  
1. JSON document:  
   • Path: /home/user/data/orders.json  
2. JSON Schema (Draft-07):  
   • Path: /home/user/data/order_schema.json  

Goal  
Create two new plain-text artefacts inside /home/user/output/ (create the directory if it doesn’t exist):

A. /home/user/output/schema_validation.log  
   • Must contain exactly the single word (all caps)  
     VALID  
   • End the file with a single trailing newline.  
   • The word VALID must appear **alone**; no extra spaces, no prefixes, no timestamps.

B. /home/user/output/high_value_customer_emails.txt  
   • Must list, one per line, the email addresses of every order that satisfies BOTH of the following jq filters:  
     1. .status == "processed"  
     2. .total  > 100  
   • Sort those email addresses in ascending (a-z) order before writing.  
   • File must end with a single trailing newline.  

Exact expected final contents of “high_value_customer_emails.txt” (including newlines):  
alice@example.com  
carol@example.com  

Operational hints (not mandatory commands)  
• Any CLI JSON-schema validator is acceptable (e.g. ajv-cli, jsonschema, etc.). If none is present, a tiny jq sanity check is fine—only the final log file matters.  
• Use jq to filter “/home/user/data/orders.json” and redirect output to the required file.  
• Ensure both output files have mode 0644 or less restrictive so the grader can read them.

The automated test will strictly check:  
1. That /home/user/output/schema_validation.log exists and its sole content is “VALID\n”.  
2. That /home/user/output/high_value_customer_emails.txt exists and has exactly the two lines shown above, newline-terminated.  
3. That no extra files are created in /home/user/output/.

Finish when both artefacts are present with the exact required contents.
